mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) captures contemporaneous consumer demand strength; higher demand flows through to corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are not positively correlated with contemporaneous RSAFS growth (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5%, persistent labor market slack curtails household income growth and compresses aggregate consumption expenditures, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns over the following 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of months when UNRATE > 8.5% during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of a near-zero federal funds rate and positive consumer price inflation creates a negative real interest rate environment that drives capital reallocation from fixed income to equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days only when both conditions are met jointly.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with inflation pickup
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce higher average 20-day returns than either variable alone (interaction term t-stat < 2.0 in regression) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-crisis recovery phase, month-over-month acceleration in industrial production signals a rebound in manufacturing activity and inventory restocking, which boosts cyclical earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods classified as post-crisis recovery, INDPRO acceleration does not predict positive 5-day returns (hit rate < 52% or Rank IC < 0.02) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates expanding labor income and household spending capacity, which translates into higher expected corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to exhibit positive Rank IC with 5-day forward returns (IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint amplifies the sensitivity of equity valuations to forward guidance and quantitative easing expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as investors discount a prolonged low-rate environment.
failure_condition: Periods with FEDFUNDS < 0.25% do not produce statistically higher average 60-day returns compared to periods above 0.25% (difference of means t-test p > 0.05) over the sample.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts sustain a 3-month average above 600 thousand units, the recovery in residential construction transmits upstream demand to building materials, durable goods, and consumer credit, lifting cyclical earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Observations with HOUST > 600K fail to generate positive 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances during walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a rising unemployment rate and declining real retail sales creates a joint demand-labor contraction signal that amplifies recession risk beyond either variable alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days when both series deteriorate simultaneously.
failure_condition: The joint deterioration signal does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60% of triggered months, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.05) in a 3-year rolling regression.
